axis: docker hard-landing as default — UX impact for Mac local dev

design_intent.raw44_2026_04_25: Mac lacks cgroup CPU/RAM caps; bare-Mac exec under load 445 + 18/19GB swap caused jetsam SIGKILL incidents. Resolver routes ALL Mac exec through Linux container.
design_intent.policy: no bare-Mac fallback; rc=76 if docker daemon down

darwin_bypass_marker_carve_out.out_of_carve_out: all other argv (including run-script, parse-only, build) routes through docker
darwin_bypass_marker_carve_out.darwin_native_marker: @resolver-bypass(...) header in .hexa file allows direct exec for chflags/launchctl/sandbox-exec/DYLD/SBPL/plutil/codesign tools

concrete_friction.impact: even single 'hello world' incurs container startup + bind-mount + entry resolution
concrete_friction.session_evidence: first omega_test.hexa run took 5s+ before producing any output

concrete_friction.issue: remote routing tried first, falls through to docker only on rc=64
concrete_friction.impact: ssh probes to 4 hosts (ubu1/hetzner/ubu2/htz) before docker fallback for unsynced cwd

concrete_friction.issue: darwin-bypass marker is presence-only; no per-tool override
concrete_friction.impact: users can't add custom safe-argv patterns without editing the resolver script

concrete_friction.issue: in-container hexa is Linux ELF; loses BSD-portable code paths
concrete_friction.impact: compounds with Ω-H1 — every Mac user actually runs Linux hexa via container, exposing all Linux/BSD divergences

remediation_options.approach: fast-path for syntax-check / dry-run / pure-compute scripts (no fs writes)
remediation_options.implementation: static analyzer detects scripts that don't call exec()/spawn/write_file; allows bare-Mac exec under cgroup-equivalent (rlimit)
remediation_options.cost: high — requires hexa AST analyzer + safety proof
remediation_options.risk: medium — false negatives leak unsafe exec to bare Mac

remediation_options.cost: low — replace hardcoded case stmt with file lookup
remediation_options.benefit: users add their own patterns without resolver edit

remediation_options.approach: explicit bypass via env: HEXA_BARE_MAC=1 (acknowledged-risk dev mode)
remediation_options.risk: high — re-introduces jetsam risk if user forgets
remediation_options.guardrail: log a WARNING line + write to ~/.hx/log/bare-mac-exec.log for audit

remediation_options.approach: container reuse via docker exec -d persistent worker
remediation_options.current: raw44 already does this (DOCKER_NAME=hexa-exec, infinity sleep)
remediation_options.optimization: ensure the warm-exec 50ms ceiling is actually held; benchmark

verdict: POLICY_TENSION severity=MEDIUM; safety gain (no SIGKILL) is real but ergonomic cost compounds over many small calls. Defaults reasonable; need user-extensibility hook.
